title: LC-meta Minimal Administrative Metadata
description: Minimal proof-of-concept schema for integrating the administrative metadata block of LC-meta into the metadata editor. This package is derived from the first LC-meta data sheet provided for the repository test.

sections.title: Corpus Metadata - Administrative Metadata
sections.description: Administrative metadata block from the LC-meta core metadata schema for learner corpora.

fields.title: Full name of the corpus
fields.description: Full name of the corpus. Example: International Corpus of Learner English, with multiple entries for different names in different languages.

fields.title: Corpus acronym
fields.description: Corpus acronym. Example: ICLE.

fields.title: Corpus version
fields.description: Corpus version. One possible approach is major.minor.patch, where major indicates new data or annotations and minor indicates corrections.

fields.title: Persistent identifier
fields.description: Persistent identifier for the corpus. Typically a DOI or Handle, for example http://hdl.handle.net/20.500.12124/25.

fields.title: Corpus citation
fields.description: Recommended citation for the corpus. Example: Author(s) or Responsible(s) (Year). Title of the data set (Version number): Distributor, Identifier or Location.

fields.title: Reference article
fields.description: Reference articles typically contain an extensive verbal description of the corpus and its design.

fields.title: Short description of the corpus
fields.description: Short description of the corpus.

fields.title: Link to more extensive corpus documentation
fields.description: Link to more extensive corpus documentation. Example: https://www.porta.eurac.edu/lci/leonide/.

fields.title: Corpus creator
fields.description: Corpus creator. Example: Max Musterman.

fields.title: Corpus contributor
fields.description: Corpus contributor. Example: Jane Doe (transcription of English texts).

fields.title: Contact e-mail address
fields.description: Contact e-mail address. Example: porta@eurac.edu.

fields.title: Name of related research project
fields.description: Name of related research project. Example: MERLIN.

fields.title: Link to related research project
fields.description: Link to related research project. Relevant when a related research project is specified. Example: https://merlin-platform.eu/.

fields.title: Person or entity responsible for the corpus
fields.description: Person or entity responsible for the corpus. Example: Institute for Applied Linguistics, Eurac Research.

fields.title: Licensing terms and conditions
fields.description: Access category for the corpus.

fields.title: Access requirements
fields.description: Access requirements when availability is restricted. Example: academic use.

fields.title: Licence name
fields.description: Licence name. Example: ACA-BY-NC-NORED.

fields.title: Link to licence text
fields.description: Link to the licence text. Example: http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/.

fields.title: Date of publication
fields.description: Date of publication. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD.

fields.title: Associated version
fields.description: Associated version. Example: 1.0, link to version.

fields.title: Character encoding
fields.description: Character encoding. Example: utf-8, latin, ascii.

fields.title: File format
fields.description: File format. Example: txt, csv, tiff, xml, JSON.

fields.title: File format specification
fields.description: File format specification. Example: TEI P5 or corpus-specific format manuals.
